title: Forced Medical Procedure Without Narrow Public-Health Justification

violations.source: Article V Section 4.1, Section 4.2, and Section 4.3
violations.summary: State law compels a non-emergency medical procedure as a condition of continued public-school attendance without a sufficiently tailored public-health justification, medical exemption structure, or individualized necessity. Article V protects bodily autonomy and bars compulsory medical decision-making absent a compelling interest pursued by the least restrictive means.

satisfied_duties.duty: block enforcement of the compulsory medical procedure law unless the state satisfies Article V's strict bodily-autonomy and public-health requirements
satisfied_duties.outcome: blocked enforcement of the compulsory medical-procedure law after finding that the state had not shown a sufficiently tailored public-health necessity or adequate medical exemptions

ai_summary.primary_violation: State law compels a non-emergency medical procedure as a condition of continued public-school attendance without a sufficiently tailored public-health justification, medical exemption structure, or individualized necessity. Article V protects bodily autonomy and bars compulsory medical decision-making absent a compelling interest pursued by the least restrictive means.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
